mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. This interaction effect produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-decline instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above 104.0 (index 2017=100) signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 104.0 are not significantly higher than when INDPRO <= 104.0 (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening that increases wage pressures and inflation expectations, which the Fed must counter with rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 1 month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of co-movement instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 0.5% signals accelerating inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise the federal funds rate, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following CPI spikes > 0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1500 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or housing cycle contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when HOUST < 1500 are not significantly lower than when HOUST >= 1500 (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in real GDP growth and retail sales signals robust aggregate demand that increases corporate revenue and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both demand indicators strengthen simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% annualized and RSAFS rises month-over-month
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-expansion instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.
